description: Filter, compare, and reshape tabular records.

tools.description:
Filters a list of tabular records, keeping only rows whose named field matches a comparison operator.

When to use: Use when the user asks to narrow, subset, where-clause, or keep-only records matching a condition (amount over 1000, status equals 'open', name contains 'acme', regex match). Supports `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `contains`, `startswith`, `regex`, `in`, `is_null`, `not_null`.

How to discover inputs: If records live in a catalog volume or workspace and the user hasn't named the path, FIRST call CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes', ...) or CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri=...) to find the file, then pass it as `source_uri`. Prefer the URI form (`master:cat.sch.vol:/file.json` or `workspace:/path/file.csv`) over inline `data`.

How to communicate: Report the count kept vs. dropped and show a few sample matching rows. Do not dump the entire result set.

Example: user says 'show invoices over $1000 from the Q3 export'. CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri='master:fin.fin.exports:/') -> FilterTool(source_uri='master:fin.fin.exports:/q3_invoices.csv', field='amount', operator='gt', value='1000').

schema.name: data
schema.description: Inline records to filter, as a JSON array of objects. Use only when records are small and already in hand; for files in AIDP storage use `source_uri` instead. Ignored when `source_uri` is set. Example: `[{"amount":1200,"status":"open"},{"amount":50,"status":"closed"}]`.

schema.name: field
schema.description: The record field name to test on each row. Must exist on the input objects. Example: `amount`, `status`, `customer.name`.

schema.name: operator
schema.description: The comparison to apply. Use `eq`/`ne` for equality, `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte` for numeric or date ordering, `contains`/`startswith` for substring, `regex` for pattern match, `in` when `value` is a JSON array, `is_null`/`not_null` for presence checks (no `value` needed).

schema.name: value
schema.description: The value to compare each record's field against. Strings are compared as-is; numbers and booleans are coerced. For `in`, pass a JSON array string like `["open","pending"]`. Not needed for `is_null` / `not_null`.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified AIDP URI to read input records from. Overrides the inline `data` field. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` for catalog volumes or `workspace:/<path>` for the user workspace. File extension drives parsing: `.json`, `.jsonl`, or `.csv`. Prefer this URI form over inline `data` for any non-trivial dataset.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Optional AIDP URI to write the filter result to. Same URI grammar as `source_uri` (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). Extension drives serialization: `.json` writes the full envelope, `.jsonl`/`.csv` writes only the matched rows. The structured envelope is still returned to the caller. Optional; omit to keep results in-memory only.

_confDescriptions.max_results: Cap on the number of matching records returned. Prevents oversized payloads back to the agent.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional AIDP URI to read input records from instead of the inline 'data' field. Supports master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> and workspace:/<path>.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional AIDP URI to write the filter result to. Extension (.json/.jsonl/.csv) controls the serialization.

tools.description:
Diffs two record sets matched on a key field and reports `added`, `removed`, and `changed` rows with old/new values per field.

When to use: Use when the user asks to compare, diff, reconcile, find changes, or detect drift between two snapshots (yesterday vs. today, baseline vs. current, source vs. target).

How to discover inputs: If the snapshots live in AIDP storage and the user hasn't named the paths, FIRST call CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri='master:cat.sch.vol:/') or WorkspaceFileTool(operation='list', ...) to locate them, then pass `left_uri` and `right_uri`. Prefer the URI form (`master:cat.sch.vol:/file.json` or `workspace:/path/file.csv`) over inline `left`/`right`.

How to communicate: Report the counts (added/removed/changed) and show a handful of representative changed rows. Don't dump every row.

Example: user says 'diff yesterday's customers against today's'. CompareTool(left_uri='workspace:/snapshots/customers_2026-06-15.json', right_uri='workspace:/snapshots/customers_2026-06-16.json', key='customer_id').

schema.name: left
schema.description: Inline left/old/baseline record set as a JSON array of objects. Use only for small in-hand data; for stored files use `left_uri`. Ignored when `left_uri` or a paired `source_uri` is set. Example: `[{"id":1,"name":"Acme"}]`.

schema.name: right
schema.description: Inline right/new/current record set as a JSON array of objects. Use only for small in-hand data; for stored files use `right_uri`. Ignored when `right_uri` or a paired `source_uri` is set. Example: `[{"id":1,"name":"Acme Inc"}]`.

schema.name: key
schema.description: The field name used to match rows across the two sets. Rows present on one side only become `added` or `removed`; rows on both sides with differing other fields become `changed`. Example: `customer_id`, `sku`, `id`.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified AIDP URI to a single JSON document of the form `{"left":[...],"right":[...]}`. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` or `workspace:/<path>`. Use this when both snapshots are bundled into one file; otherwise use `left_uri`/`right_uri` for split inputs.

schema.name: left_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified AIDP URI to read the left/old record set from. Takes precedence over both `source_uri` and inline `left`. Same URI grammar as the other URIs (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). File extension drives parsing: `.json`, `.jsonl`, `.csv`. Prefer this URI form over inline `left` for any non-trivial dataset.

schema.name: right_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified AIDP URI to read the right/new record set from. Takes precedence over both `source_uri` and inline `right`. Same URI grammar (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). File extension drives parsing: `.json`, `.jsonl`, `.csv`. Prefer this URI form over inline `right`.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Optional AIDP URI to write the diff output to. Same URI form (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). `.json` writes the full envelope (`added`/`removed`/`changed`/`summary`); `.jsonl`/`.csv` writes only the `changed` rows. The structured envelope is still returned to the caller. Optional; omit to keep results in-memory only.

_confDescriptions.tolerance: Numeric equality tolerance for float field comparisons. Values within this delta are treated as equal.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional AIDP URI to a paired {left, right} JSON document. Use left_uri/right_uri instead for split inputs.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional AIDP URI to write the diff output to. Extension (.json/.jsonl/.csv) controls the serialization.

tools.description:
Reshapes tabular records via `select`, `rename`, `sort`, `dedupe`, or `groupby` aggregation.

When to use: Use when the user asks to pick columns, rename fields, sort, drop duplicates, summarize, count by group, sum/avg by category, or otherwise restructure data between flow steps. Pair with FilterTool when both subsetting and reshaping are needed.

How to discover inputs: If records live in AIDP storage and the user hasn't named the path, FIRST call CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri='master:cat.sch.vol:/') or WorkspaceFileTool(operation='list', ...) to locate the file, then pass it as `source_uri`. Prefer the URI form (`master:cat.sch.vol:/file.csv` or `workspace:/path/file.json`) over inline `data`.

How to communicate: Report the resulting row/column count and show a few sample rows. Do not echo the entire reshaped table.

Example: user says 'total revenue by region from the orders export'. DataManipulationTool(source_uri='workspace:/exports/orders.csv', operation='groupby', spec='{"by":["region"],"agg":{"revenue":"sum"}}').

schema.name: data
schema.description: Inline records to reshape, as a JSON array of objects. Use only for small in-hand data; for stored files use `source_uri`. Ignored when `source_uri` is set. Example: `[{"region":"NA","revenue":100}]`.

schema.name: operation
schema.description: The reshape action. Use `select` to keep a subset of columns, `rename` to relabel fields, `sort` to order rows, `dedupe` to drop duplicate rows, `groupby` to aggregate by one or more keys.

schema.name: spec
schema.description: Operation parameters as a JSON object string. Shape depends on `operation`: select=`{"columns":["a","b"]}`, rename=`{"map":{"old":"new"}}`, sort=`{"by":["field"],"ascending":true}`, dedupe=`{"subset":["id"]}`, groupby=`{"by":["region"],"agg":{"revenue":"sum"}}`. Always pass valid JSON.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified AIDP URI to read input records from. Overrides the inline `data` field. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` for catalog volumes or `workspace:/<path>` for the user workspace. File extension drives parsing: `.json`, `.jsonl`, or `.csv`. Prefer this URI form over inline `data` for any non-trivial dataset.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Optional AIDP URI to write the reshaped rows to. Same URI grammar as `source_uri` (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). `.json` writes the full envelope; `.jsonl`/`.csv` writes only the reshaped rows. The structured envelope is still returned to the caller. Optional; omit to keep results in-memory only.

_confDescriptions.max_rows: Cap on rows returned after the operation. Protects the agent context from large result sets.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional AIDP URI to read input records from instead of the inline 'data' field. Supports master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> and workspace:/<path>.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional AIDP URI to write the reshaped rows to. Extension (.json/.jsonl/.csv) controls the serialization.
